title: Write ops: sheet management
description: Add add_sheet(), delete_sheet(), and rename_sheet() to ops/write.py. add_sheet() accepts --position (1-based, default end) and raises an error on duplicate name. delete_sheet() raises an error if removing the sheet would leave zero sheets. rename_sheet() raises an error if old name is missing or new name already exists. Wire 'pyxlsx sheet add/delete/rename' CLI commands in cli.py.
acceptance_criteria: add_sheet() inserts a blank sheet at the specified 1-based position and errors on duplicate name
delete_sheet() removes the named sheet and errors if it is the last sheet
rename_sheet() renames correctly and errors on missing old name or duplicate new name
pyxlsx sheet add/delete/rename output correct confirmation JSON
All error paths write to stderr and exit with code 1
All writes are atomic via temp file + os.replace()

handoff_summary.completed:
- add_sheet(file, name, position=None): inserts blank sheet at 1-based position (default end), errors on duplicate name via sys.exit(1)
- delete_sheet(file, name): removes sheet, errors if sheet not found or it is the last sheet
- rename_sheet(file, old_name, new_name): renames sheet, errors if old_name missing or new_name already exists
- All three functions use atomic save (mkstemp + os.replace)
- cli.py: imported add_sheet/delete_sheet/rename_sheet; added sheet add/delete/rename sub-commands under the existing `sheet` group with correct JSON and --plain output
handoff_summary.remaining: None — all acceptance criteria addressed in ops layer and CLI layer.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. The openpyxl create_sheet/delete/rename APIs are stable and the atomic-write pattern is consistent with set_cell().
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should cover: add_sheet at default/explicit/out-of-range positions; duplicate-name error; delete_sheet happy path and last-sheet guard; rename_sheet happy path, missing old name, duplicate new name; CLI sheet add/delete/rename JSON and --plain output; error exit codes.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No overlapping file edits with sibling beads expected; write.py and cli.py are owned solely by this bead within the feature tree.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used openpyxl create_sheet(index=None) for default-end behaviour; computed actual_position from wb.sheetnames.index() after insertion so the returned position is always authoritative. Arrow character in rename --plain output encoded as \u2192 to avoid any encoding issues.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tests for all three functions and their three CLI commands should be added to tests/test_xlsx_write.py and tests/test_xlsx_cli.py by the tester agent.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: openpyxl sheet name length limit (31 chars) and forbidden characters are not validated at the CLI layer — openpyxl itself will raise if violated, which will surface as an unhandled exception rather than a clean error message. This is acceptable per spec scope.

conflict_risks: No overlapping file edits with sibling beads expected; write.py and cli.py are owned solely by this bead within the feature tree.

execution_history.summary: Added add_sheet(), delete_sheet(), and rename_sheet() to ops/write.py, and wired pyxlsx sheet add/delete/rename CLI commands in cli.py. All writes use the same atomic temp-file + os.replace() pattern as set_cell(). Error paths print to stderr and call sys.exit(1). Both files pass py_compile.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
